problem_addressed: Existing multi-domain architectures for skin lesion segmentation inadequately utilize prediction uncertainty, leading to blind fusion of cross-domain artifacts, passive topological reasoning, and rigid optimization that overfits to label noise.
core_claim: UGDD-Net transforms uncertainty into an active guiding signal throughout the entire learning lifecycle, enabling more reliable skin lesion segmentation through uncertainty-in-the-loop design.
key_contributions: Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Network (UGDD-Net) with unified architecture
Three uncertainty-guided modules: UGBFF, UGGR, and UGML
novelty: Active integration of uncertainty as a guiding signal throughout the learning process, rather than treating it as a passive post-hoc metric, combined with dual-domain (spatial-spectral) architecture for skin lesion segmentation.
method_summary: UGDD-Net employs a two-pass forward mechanism where the first pass generates initial predictions and uncertainty estimates, and the second pass refines features using uncertainty-guided fusion, graph-based refinement, and adaptive loss weighting.
key_innovation: The 'Glance-and-Gaze' mechanism that actively uses pixel-level uncertainty to dynamically modulate spatial-spectral interactions and guide global reasoning through priority-based graph propagation.
assumptions: Prediction uncertainty can be reliably estimated from model outputs
Uncertainty correlates with segmentation difficulty and domain shift
Domain-invariant structural features can be extracted through frequency domain processing
theoretical_grounding: Empirical validation through statistical significance testing (p < 0.01) and extensive cross-dataset evaluations, though no formal proofs or theoretical derivations are provided.
headline_result: UGDD-Net achieves IoUcon of 73.91% and Dicecon of 83.37% against Expert Consensus
ablations: Loss 3: Dice + CE + Boundary (adds boundary-aware penalty)
improvement_over_baseline: UGML (Loss 4) improves over Loss 3: IoU increases from 91.84 to 92.61, Dice from 95.52 to 96.01, HD95 decreases from 7.05 to 6.45, ECE decreases from 2.30 to 2.07. UGDD-Net improves over UDEL + TTA: IoUcon increases from 70.21 to 73.91, Dicecon from 80.23 to 83.37, Kappa from 0.6534 to 0.6906, Pearson from 0.4876 to 0.5291, Diceunc from 67.29 to 71.56
best_result: UGDD-Net achieves IoUcon of 73.91% and Dicecon of 83.37% against Expert Consensus
title: Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Learning for Reliable Skin Lesion Segmentation
